key_insights.insight: Cortisol is a critical hormone to balance, acting like a dictator in the body, influencing immune function, blood sugar, and stress response.
key_insights.quote: Cortisol is more like a dictator, especially if it's out of whack...cortisol is critical in terms of helping you with your immune system, helping you with your blood sugar, and just managing the stress response.

key_insights.insight: Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs) can have long-lasting impacts on physical health, increasing the risk of chronic diseases by influencing the immune, nervous, and endocrine systems.
key_insights.quote: If you know that you have an elevated ACE score...there's a way that those ACEs are living on in your body unless you're addressing them.

key_insights.insight: Conventional medicine often focuses on treating symptoms with pharmaceuticals and fails to address the root causes of disease through lifestyle changes.
key_insights.quote: 70% of the diseases we're facing right now are utterly preventable with lifestyle medicine.

key_insights.insight: Precision medicine focuses on individual genomic blueprints, biomarkers, and wearable data to personalize treatment plans and interventions.
key_insights.quote: Precision medicine is where we understand you as an individual.We look at your genomic blueprint.We look at your biomarkers.We look at your wearables data to determine NF1 experiments where you serve as your own control.

key_insights.insight: Gut health is crucial for hormone balance, as certain gut bacteria can recirculate estrogen, increasing the risk of hormone-related cancers and metabolic dysfunction.
key_insights.quote: There are three bacteria in the gut that can take estrogen and make it keep recirculating...those people tend to have higher estrogen levels.

key_insights.insight: Women in perimenopause often feel dismissed by the medical system, despite experiencing a wide range of symptoms beyond just hormonal changes.
key_insights.quote: 73 to 75% of women do not get the treatment for perimenopause and menopause that they deserve.

key_insights.insight: The women's health gap is rooted in both sex differences (hormones) and gender differences (socially constructed stressors).
key_insights.quote: There's sex differences that map to those outcomes, but then there's these gender differences.In the way that women don't feel supported, the way that they feel conflicted in trying to create work-life balance, the way that they experience more stress, that's whatwe need to address.

key_insights.insight: Sleep is critical for hormonal balance, with even one bad night impacting insulin, cortisol, hunger, and carbohydrate cravings.
key_insights.quote: One bad night of sleep raises your insulin, raises your cortisol the next day, makes you more hungry, makes you more likely to crave carbohydrates.

actionable_advice.advice: Address elevated cortisol through top-down (cognitive) and bottom-up (sensory) approaches.
actionable_advice.how_to: Practice breathwork, meditation, rhythmic movements (dancing, walking, hiking). Consider supplements like cortisol manager (ashwagandha, phosphatidylserine) or Rhodiola.

actionable_advice.advice: Measure your homocysteine levels to assess heart-specific inflammation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Request a basic blood panel from your doctor. Aim for a homocysteine level between 5-7. If elevated, consider supplementing with methylated B vitamins.

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize fiber intake to support gut health and hormone balance.
actionable_advice.how_to: Consume 30-40 grams of fiber daily from real food sources, aiming for 25-35 different species of fruits and vegetables per week. Focus on broccoli, brussels sprouts and other cruciferous vegetables.

actionable_advice.advice: Consider a continuous glucose monitor (CGM) for real-time feedback on your food choices.
actionable_advice.how_to: Wear a small patch that measures blood sugar levels and transmits data to your phone. Use this information to adjust your diet and lifestyle.

actionable_advice.advice: Get morning sunlight to regulate your circadian rhythm.
actionable_advice.how_to: Spend at least 30 minutes outside in the morning tracing your eyes along the horizon.

memorable_stories: Dr. Sal's personal experience with undiagnosed pre-diabetes and high cortisol levels despite being a physician.
The NBA players experiencing low testosterone in the 'bubble' due to isolation and stress.
Dr. Sal's journey through divorce and the concept of the 'whole body yes'.
contrarian_claims: Mainstream medicine is broken and prioritizes profit over optimal health.
Hormone levels vary too much is often used as a reason to not measure them, which is a double standard.
Birth control pills are overused and people do not receive full informed consent about side effects.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Menopause Expert: Belly Fat Grows During Menopause! Your Estrogen Levels Are Controlling You & This Hormone Is Quietly Killing Your Sex Life!
